ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a masterpiece of practical effects and paranoia-driven horror. Praised for its tense atmosphere, strong characters, and iconic ending. Mentioned multiple times as a top recommendation for both horror fans and newcomers to the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a generation-defining horror film that terrified audiences. Praised for its heavy atmosphere, iconic villain (Samara), and its cultural impact, including making TV static unnerving. Multiple users shared personal stories of how it scared them.

ranked_movies.title: The Descent
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an intensely claustrophobic and nightmare-inducing film. Users specifically praised its blend of real-world survival horror with supernatural elements and its brutally effective, bleak ending.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a modern horror masterpiece that is both terrifying and emotionally devastating. Recognized for its powerful performances, slow-building dread, and being one of the few recent films to genuinely scare experienced horror fans in a theater setting.

ranked_movies.title: Sinister
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically praised for its 'Super 8' film segments and unsettling soundtrack, which were described as 'fundamentally unsettling' and the scariest parts of the film, leaving a lasting chilling effect on viewers.

ranked_movies.title: The Conjuring
ranked_movies.reason: The entire franchise was discussed as a cultural phenomenon, described as the 'MCU for horror-heads'. The recent 'Last Rites' film was noted for having a record-breaking box office opening, proving the series' massive mainstream appeal.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its iconic scenes, quotes, and status as a classic. Specific shots (like Father Merrin's arrival) and lines ('What have you done to his eyes?!') were highlighted as profoundly scary and culturally significant.

ranked_movies.title: Halloween
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently referenced as a foundational slasher film. Its simple, effective premise, iconic villain (Michael Myers), and perfect ending were specifically praised across multiple discussions.

ranked_movies.title: The Wailing
ranked_movies.reason: Hailed as a phenomenal and terrifying foreign horror film. Users noted its long runtime and subtitles were worth it for a deeply scary experience that 'unlocked a new level of fear'.

ranked_movies.title: It Follows
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a modern classic with a unique premise and one of the best horror soundtracks. Notably called a 'comfort horror watch' due to its atmosphere and setting, indicating its high rewatchability.

ranked_movies.title: The Witch
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its oppressive atmosphere, historical authenticity, and lingering sense of dread. Recognized as a standout film that is both unsettling and beautifully crafted.

ranked_movies.title: 28 Days Later
ranked_movies.reason: Credited with revolutionizing the zombie/infected genre and making it 'so much more terrifying'. Its impact on horror and its intense, gritty style were frequently acknowledged.

ranked_movies.title: Paranormal Activity
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its effective found-footage style and ability to create terror with minimal elements. Specific scenes, like Katie standing still for hours, were cited as 'absolute nightmare fuel'.

ranked_movies.title: Hellraiser
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its iconic and disturbing imagery, particularly the line 'Jesus wept'. Recognized as a classic that creates a unique and lasting sense of dread.

ranked_movies.title: Drag Me to Hell
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its brutally unfair and painful ending. The film was noted for its bleakness, where the protagonist is damned for a minor mistake, making its conclusion particularly memorable.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Cited for its profoundly scary quotes ('Where we're going, we don't need eyes to see') and its reputation as a cult classic that blends sci-fi and horror effectively.

ranked_movies.title: The Blair Witch Project
ranked_movies.reason: Remembered fondly for its groundbreaking marketing and theater experience. Users shared personal stories of seeing it when it was marketed as real, making it feel like watching a 'snuff film'.

ranked_movies.title: Scream
ranked_movies.reason: Recognized as a brilliant meta-horror classic and a common 'first horror movie' for many viewers. Its ability to be both scary and fun makes it a frequent gateway into the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Mist
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its incredibly bleak and sad ending that left viewers both heartbroken and angry. Its emotional impact was a key point of discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Saint Maud
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its shocking and perfect final shot, which was described as a 'split second that changes the entire ending'. Its psychological horror and climax were highly recommended.
